description: Calculate the cost of holding perpetual futures positions. Real-time funding rate estimates for crypto leverage trading.
intro: Perpetual futures funding rates are the cost of maintaining a leveraged position on cryptocurrency exchanges. Understanding funding costs is essential for traders managing positions on platforms like Binance, Bybit, OKEx, and Kraken. This calculator applies the industry-standard formula with values current as of April 2026, reviewed periodically to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **position size × funding rate × intervals**. Direct calculation of costs to hold perpetual positions. The amount you pay or receive every 8 hours.
useCases: Technical calculations for perpetual futures funding costs
Validate expected costs before opening positions

explanation:
Perpetual futures contracts don't have an expiration date. Every 8 hours, the exchange settles funding payments between long and short positions to keep the perpetual price aligned with the spot price. This is how the market maintains balance.

## How Funding Works

When funding rates are positive, long traders pay short traders. When negative, short traders pay long traders. The funding amount is calculated as: **Position Size × Funding Rate × Number of Intervals**.

Most exchanges (Binance, Bybit, OKEx, Kraken) settle funding every 8 hours at 00:00, 08:00, and 16:00 UTC.

## Typical Funding Rate Ranges

| Market Condition | 8-Hour Rate | Annualized |
|---|---|---|
| Neutral market | 0.01% – 0.03% | 3.6% – 11% |
| Bullish (heavy longs) | 0.05% – 0.10% | 18% – 36% |
| Bearish (heavy shorts) | −0.05% – −0.10% | −18% – −36% |
| Extreme (flash crashes) | ±0.20% – 0.50% | ±73% – 182% |

## Strategic Considerations

- **High rates signal risk**: When funding spikes, one side is overpopulated. Consider contrarian positions.
- **Timing exits**: Close before settlement if rates are unsustainably high.
- **Carry trading**: Combine spot holdings with short perpetuals at high funding to generate yield without directional risk.
- **Track your costs**: $10,000 at 0.05% per 8 hours = $274/year in funding payments.

## Final Notes

This calculator is a reference guide for educational purposes. For critical financial decisions, consult a licensed financial advisor. Values are current as of April 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: Perpetual contracts don't expire. Every 8 hours, the exchange settles funding payments between longs and shorts to keep perpetual prices aligned with spot prices. Positive funding means longs pay shorts; negative means shorts pay longs.

faq.q: How often do funding payments settle?
faq.a: Most exchanges (Binance, Bybit, OKEx) settle funding every 8 hours at 00:00, 08:00, and 16:00 UTC. Some platforms offer hourly or daily settlement options.
Normal ranges from 0.01% to 0.03% per 8 hours (3–11% annualized). During strong rallies, rates can reach 0.1%+ (36%+ annualized). During crashes, rates often flip negative.
Yes. Extreme market moves like flash crashes or pump-and-dumps can push rates to 0.2–0.5% per 8 hours. Always monitor rates before opening large positions.

faq.q: How can I use funding rates in trading?
faq.a: High funding attracts yield farmers. You can sell spot Bitcoin and hold a short perpetual to collect carry yield. Or wait for rates to normalize before entering positions during funding spikes.

faq.q: Do I pay funding on spot holdings?
faq.a: No. Funding only applies to perpetual futures positions. Spot Bitcoin, Ethereum, or other cryptocurrencies have zero funding costs.

faq.q: What happens if funding is negative while I'm long?
faq.a: You receive payments instead of paying them. Negative funding means shorts outnumber longs, and you profit from the difference.

faq.q: Should I close my position before settlement?
faq.a: Only if you expect extreme rates. Most traders don't optimize this closely unless rates exceed 0.05% per interval. Use funding rate alerts from your exchange.

faq.q: How much does funding impact my returns?
faq.a: At 0.05% per 8 hours, the annual cost is ~18% on your notional position. For low-margin strategies, this is significant. Track funding in your profit-and-loss calculations.

faq.q: Which exchanges have the most stable funding?
faq.a: Tier-1 exchanges like Binance, Bybit, and OKEx typically have liquid markets with stable funding. Lesser-known exchanges can have more volatile rates and wider spreads.
